title: VddEngine::send_to_builder lacks timeout that send_to_adversary now has after #496
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/vdd/mod.rs:1278-1323, 995-1041
## Violation
send_to_adversary at line 1184-1275 wraps the request and body-read in tokio::time::timeout(adversary.request_timeout_seconds) per the comment referencing crosslink #496 fix:

```rust
let timeout_secs = self.config.adversary.request_timeout_seconds;
let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_secs(timeout_secs);
let response = tokio::time::timeout(timeout, forward_request(...)).await
    .map_err(|_| VddError::AdversaryRequestFailed(format!("adversary request timed out after {timeout_secs}s")))?
    ...
```

But the sibling methods that route through the SAME forward_request — send_to_builder (1278) and send_to_builder_for_verification (995) — have NO timeout wrap. A hung builder revision request blocks the whole VDD blocking loop indefinitely; a hung verifier request similarly blocks every iteration.

The #496 mitigation is half-applied: the adversary is timeout-protected but the verifier and builder paths share the same hung-API failure mode and got missed.

## Mandated refactor
Extract the timeout-wrapped forward_request into a helper:

```rust
async fn forward_with_timeout(
    client: &Client,
    provider: &ProviderConfig,
    provider_name: &str,
    model: &str,
    endpoint: &str,
    body: &Value,
    headers: Vec<(String, String)>,
    timeout_secs: u64,
) -> Result<reqwest::Response, VddError> { ... }
```

All three send_* methods use this helper with the same timeout config. Add a separate config field for verifier_timeout_seconds and builder_timeout_seconds if independent control is needed; otherwise reuse adversary.request_timeout_seconds for all three.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
